overview.description:
CellClick is a web-based data analysis platform with interactive functions specialized for cell type 
						identification from single-cell omics and spatial omics data. CellClick takes different types of single-cell or 
						spatial omics sequencing data (either preprocessed or un-preprocessed ) stored in the Scanpy-supported formats (e.g. h5ad format) as inputs. 
						The major feature of CellClick is to provide multiple interactive functions for users to visualize, analysis, evaluate, re-annotate or re-cluster cells. 
						It also allows parallel comparison of the analysis results for different genes, cell clusters, or datasets, which is absent in most currently available software. 
						CellClick provides the above-mentioned functions in five modules, namely Preprocessing, Data Visualization, Cell Annotation, Annotation Evaluation, and Cell Reannotation module.

This function provides multiple methods to upload the dataset. 
						The method depends on the values of Data Source input, which includes 'Local Data', 'History Data', and 'Current Data'.

inputs.Data Source: Define the data source of data to upload.
inputs.Local Data: Upload the dataset from local storage. Visible when setting 'Data Source' as 'Local Data'.
inputs.History Data: Upload the dataset which has been analysed with CellClick. Visible when setting 'Data Source' as 'History Data'.

example.title: Upload data by local data
example.description:
By clicking the 'Upload Data' button, you can upload your dataset directly from your local storage. 
							CellClick will assign a unique CellClick ID to each uploaded dataset via the 'Local Data' method.

example.title: Upload data by history data
example.description: CellClick will store the uploaded dataset and allow you to re-upload it via the 'History Data' method.

overview.description: CellClick sets the maximum (5 by default) for uploading dataset and this function allows you to remove dataset from CellClick.

inputs.Data ID: The ID of dataset you want to remove.

overview.description: If you have uploaded multiple datasets, this function allows you to change the current dataset.

inputs.Data ID: The ID of dataset you want to analyze.

overview.description:
CellClick classifies the feature stored in adata.obs as 'Category' and 'Number', 
						and this function allows you to change the classification of cell features.

inputs.Data Type: The data type of the annotation.

overview.description: This function allows you to filter cells based on UMI counts, gene counts, and the percent of mitochondrial genes with interactive violin plots.

inputs.Attributes: The cell attributes involved in QC.

example.title: QC cells based on UMI counts, gene counts, and the percent of mitochondrial genes
example.description:
After visualizing the QC graphs, you can slide the slider to set the threshold for the corresponding cell attribute. 
							The input component shows the corresponding or total counts of cells to filter. 
							Once you ensure the threshold, you can filter cells by clicking the 'Filter Cells' button.

description: Normalize each cell by total counts over all genes and logarithmize the normalized counts matrix. Refer to

inputs.Target Sum: The total counts over all genes to normalize each cell.
inputs.Exclude Highly Expressed: Whether to exclude very highly expressed genes when normalize each cell.
inputs.Log1P: Whether to logarithmize the normalized counts matrix.

example.title: The normalization, HVG detection, and dimension reduction analysis in CellClick
example.description: You will be informed by the Alter component if the analysis is completed.

description: Annotate highly variable genes. Refer to

inputs.Flavor: Choose the flavor for identifying highly variable genes.
inputs.Top Genes: Number of highly-variable genes to keep.

example.title: The normalization, HVG detection, and dimension reduction analysis in CellClick
example.description: You will be informed by the Alter component if the analysis is completed.

description: Reduce the data dimension and embed cells into 2-dimensional space. Refer to the functions from the

inputs.Method: The method to reduce dimension and embed cells, including PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP.
inputs.N PCs: The number of PC used for PCA and the calculation of KNN graph (Method='UMAP').
inputs.N Neighbors: The size of local neighborhood used for UMAP. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP'.
inputs.Min Dist: The effective minimum distance between embedded points. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP'.
inputs.Metric: The metric name used for distance computation. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP' or 't-SNE'.

example.title: The normalization, HVG detection, and dimension reduction analysis in CellClick
example.description: You will be informed by the Alter component if the analysis is completed.

description:
CellClick identifies cell types based on cell clustering results or the original annotation. 
							So you need to cluster cells for un-preprocessed data or set the original annotation result for preprocessed data. 
							For un-preprocessed data, the Leiden or Louvain algorithms are provided for cell clustering, 
							and the results will be stored with the prefix 'CellClick_'. Refer to

inputs.Preprocessed: 'Custom' and 'CellClic' are available in this input. 'Custom' means preprocessed data, while 'CellClick' means un-preprocessed data.
inputs.Reference: The column name of original annotation result stored in adata.obs. Visible when setting 'Processed By' as 'Custom'.
inputs.Method: Leiden or Louvain algorithm used to cluster cell. Visible when setting 'Processed By' as 'CellClick'.
inputs.Resolution: The resolution for cell clustering results. Visible when setting 'Processed By' as 'CellClick'.

Data Visualization.overview: CellClick supports interactive visualization functions to facilitate cell embedding, gene embedding, and gene dot graph generation.

overview.description: Cell embedding empowers direct visualization of cell features in low-dimensional space, which is essential for cell type identification.

inputs.Embedding: The space name used to embed cells.

example.title: Cell selection on cell embedding results
example.description:
You can select cells in the embedding results via box selection or lasso selection icons, 
							then employ the selected cells for different downstream analysis. 
							By clicking on the legend icon of each cell cluster, the target cell cluster can be hidden or redisplayed, 
							thus facilitate you to visualize and select specific cell sets more accurately.

overview.description: The gene embedding function displays gene expression profiles in embedding space, which provides direct evidence for cell type identification.

inputs.Embedding: The space name used to embed cells.

example.title: Gene embedding
example.description: The expression profile of the target gene is showed in the embedding space.

overview.description: This function visualize the expression of multiple genes, which provides statistical evidence for cell type identification.

example.title: Gene dot
example.description: In the dot plot, you can add or delete gene in 'Gene Name' input.

Gene Analysis.overview:
The Cell Annotation module aims to provide fast and accurate cell identification methods, 
			which include Marker Gene Identification and Cell Identification functions.

description:
Maker gene identification is a basic and crucial step for single-cell or spatial omics sequencing data analysis. 
							To facilitate fast and accurate marker gene identification, CellClick implemented
, an efficient and accurate cell marker gene identification algorithm based on cosine similarity.

inputs.Annotation: The key of the cell groups in adata.obs.
inputs.Group A:
The cell group(s) for marker gene identification. If 'selected', CellClick will identify the marker gene for selected cells and group B. 
							If 'all', 'none' is the only valid value for the groupB and CellClick will identify the marker gene for each cell group.
inputs.Group B:
The reference cell group(s) for marker gene identification. If 'rest', CellClick will compare the groupA with all other cells. 
							If 'none', CellClick will identify the marker gene for each cell group in the group A.

example.title: Marker gene identification
example.description:
Only when the group buttons are both inactive, is the submit button accessible to click. 
							When the group button is inactive, the group input is disabled. 
							The Group B button is inactive when the Group A button is active.

overview.description:
Cell Identification function provides a fast method to annotate cell type based on identified marker genes and reference marker genes, 
						which considers the percent of information used for querying and the sum of confidence calculated by the specificity and sensitivity of identified marker genes. 
						For more accurate cell identification, the Cell Identification function generates a dot plot to visualize the expression pattern of identified marker genes 
						and the overlap between identified marker genes and reference marker genes.

inputs.Marker Source: The source of the reference marker genes.
inputs.Species: Obtain the reference marker genes from the special species.
inputs.Cluster Name: The cell cluster for Cell Identification function.
inputs.Gene Number: The counts of identified marker gene used for Cell Identification function.

example.title: Cell Identification
example.description:
In the results of the Cell Identification function, the bar plot shows the gene scores of different cell types in reference data. 
							In the dot plot, the genes colored by red are the overlap genes between identified genes 
							and suggested marker genes of cell type selected by 'Cell Type' input, 
							while the genes colored by blue are the unique genes of identified genes. 
							You can annotate the cell type directly in the Cell Identification function result by clicking the 'Annotation' button.

Evaluation and Validation.overview:
The Annotation Validation module includes Annotation Evaluation and Reference Comparison functions, 
			which allows users to evaluation the cell annotation results.

overview.description:
The Annotation Evaluation function invokes Gene Dot and Gene Embedding functions to 
						display the expression profiles of identified marker genes for a chosen cell type.

inputs.Cell Cluster: The cell cluster for Annotation Evaluation function.
inputs.Gene Number: The counts of identified marker gene used for Annotation Evaluation function.
inputs.Embedding: The space name used to embed cells.

overview.description:
The Reference Comparison function measures the similarity based on gene similarity, relative expression specificity, 
						and expression abundance difference between identified marker genes and suggested marker genes. 
						Similar to the Cell Identification function, Reference Comparison function also provides the dot plot for users to 
						evaluate the results the confidence of the Reference Comparison function results, which shows the expression pattern of suggested marker genes.

inputs.Marker Source:
Besides pre-uploaded marker gene reference, CellClick allows custom marker gene reference formatted as JSON file. 
							You can store it in the 'marker_ref/Other' directory and select 'Other'. 
							And, you can also select other uploaded data by selecting 'Upload Data' and identify its marker genes as marker gene reference.
inputs.Source Name: The tissue name, custom marker gene reference name, or data ID for cluster evaluation.
inputs.Cell Cluster: The cell cluster for cluster evaluation.
inputs.Gene Number: The number of reference marker gene used used for cluster evaluation.

example.title: Reference Comparison
example.description:
In the results of the Reference Comparison function, the violin plot shows the cluster scores of suggested marker genes from different cell types. 
							Generally, a high cluster score distribution shows suggested marker genes highly and specifically expressed in the cell cluster, 
							which always demonstrates a high confidence to annotate cell clusters as the corresponding cell type. 
							The dot plot shows the expression pattern of the suggested marker genes from the cell type selected by 'Cell Type' input. 
							You can annotate the cell type directly in the Reference Comparison function by clicking the 'Annotation' button.

Cell Reannotation.overview:
The Cell Reannotation module incorporates Cell Selection, Cell Reclustering, Cluster Reannotation, 
				and Annotation Update functions, which facilitate users to select target cells for refined annotation.

overview.description:
Cell reannotation requires the accurate selection for a special cell set, 
						which relies on many factors, such as cell cluster, gene expression, and coordinate information in embedding space, 
						and remains a challenge in most available software. 
						CellClick has developed two cell selection methods with cell visualization result and cell table.

inputs.Embedding: The space name used to embed cells.
inputs.Table Content: Metadata or gene expression showed in Cell Table.

example.title: Cell selection on cell embedding results
example.description:
You can select cells in the embedding results via box selection or lasso selection icons, 
							then employ the selected cells for different downstream analysis. By clicking on the legend icon of each cell cluster, 
							the target cell cluster can be hidden or redisplayed, thus facilitate you to visualize and select specific cell sets more accurately.

example.title: Cell Table Introduction
example.description:
Cell Table is an interactive table for cell querying based on cell attributes. 
							You can show the data frame stored in adata.obs or adata.X by change the value of 'Table Content' input. 
							And you are allowed to add or remove column of showing information by 'Add Column' and 'Remove Column' input.

example.title: Cell selection on Cell Table
example.description:
You can query and select cells by attributes in the Cell Table, 
							for example, selecting a special cell cluster based on cell cluster results.

example.title: Cell selection flow in CellClick
example.description:
The selection results can be fixed with 'Fix Selection' button, and then be merged with novel cell selection results. 
							The calculation of difference, union, and intersection set between two cell selection results 
							are supported by CellClick with plus, minus, and heart button.

overview.description: Cluster Reannotation function provides a direct method to annotate cell sets.

inputs.Raw Name: The raw name of cell cluster.
inputs.New Name: The new name of cell cluster.

overview.description: You are allowed to re-cluster the target cell set with the Re-clustering function.

inputs.Cluster: The cell cluster to re-cluster. If 'selected', CellClick will re-cluster the selected cells.
inputs.Dimension Reduction: Display or hide the inputs about dimension reduction. If un-active, dimension reduction analysis will be disabled.
inputs.dimension_Method: The method to reduce dimension and embed cells, including PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP.
inputs.N PCs: The number of PC used for PCA and the calculation of KNN graph (Method='UMAP').
inputs.N Neighbors: The size of local neighborhood used for UMAP. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP'.
inputs.Min Dist: The effective minimum distance between embedded points. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP'.
inputs.Metric: The metric name used for distance computation. Visible when setting 'Method' as 'UMAP' or 't-SNE'.
inputs.Cell Reclustering: Display or hide the inputs about dimension reduction.
inputs.Method: The cluster algorithm (Louvain or Leiden) used to clustering cells.
inputs.Resolution: The resolution for cell clustering results.

overview.description: After reannotating the reclustered cells, you can run the Annotation Update function to merge the newly annotated cell clusters with other cell clusters.

example.title: Annotation Update
example.description: the Annotation Update function replaces the cell annotation from source data with the annotation of cells from subset data.
